summary.intent: Update the openclaw import module to support sub-agent importing now that Moltis has sub-agent support via SpawnAgent, removing a hardcoded TODO that was previously accurate but became outdated.
summary.outcome: Added model extraction to ImportedAgent, implemented agents_to_presets() conversion, persisted non-default agents as AgentPreset entries in moltis.toml, removed the stale TODO, updated docs, added 4 tests, and committed/pushed all changes.

repo: The openclaw-import crate lives at crates/openclaw-import/ and has separate modules: lib.rs (orchestration), agents.rs (agent extraction), types.rs (OpenClaw schema), workspace_files.rs
Moltis sub-agent support is implemented via crates/tools/src/spawn_agent.rs with nesting depth up to 3 and delegate toolsets
MoltisConfig.agents is an AgentsConfig with presets: HashMap<String, AgentPreset>; presets are keyed by moltis_id
moltis-config is already a dependency of openclaw-import, so no Cargo.toml changes were needed
The codebase uses a pinned nightly Rust toolchain for formatting
CLAUDE.md requires keeping docs in sync with code changes — docs/src/openclaw-import.md must be updated when import behavior changes

code.finding: Hardcoded TODO 'Sub-agents not supported' was outdated — SpawnAgent existed but import hadn't been updated to use it
ImportedAgent struct was missing a model field; OpenClawAgentEntry.model supports both Simple(string) and Full({primary, fallbacks}) variants that needed to be handled
synthesize_from_detection also needed the new model field added alongside extract_from_config_list to avoid compile errors

workflow: Pattern: check both sides of an integration (Moltis AgentPreset schema + OpenClaw config format) before implementing a mapping
Re-import safety: merging presets without overwriting existing ones is a deliberate idempotency design — existing user customizations are preserved
Test coverage split: unit tests in agents.rs for conversion logic, integration tests in lib.rs for end-to-end persistence and idempotency

friction: Multiple grep iterations were needed to locate subagent-related code because the codebase used inconsistent naming (sub-agent, subagent, spawn_agent)
The synthesize_from_detection function also needed the model field update, which was only discovered at compile time — not caught during the initial struct change
Finding the end of the test module in lib.rs required multiple Read calls at different offsets due to file length
open_items: OpenClaw sub-agent delegation config (if it exists beyond top-level agent definitions) is not yet mapped — only top-level non-default agents are converted to presets
The AgentPreset mapping is minimal (name, theme, model) — other potential fields from OpenClaw agents (tools, system prompt, etc.) are not yet imported
No migration path for users who already imported and got the 'Sub-agents not supported' TODO — they'd need to re-import to get presets
